question_text: A car with a mass of 1200 kg is travelling at constant speed on a horizontal road. The engine provides a driving force of 1500 N. Which statement best explains why the car maintains constant speed rather than accelerating?

options.label: A
options.text: The friction and air resistance forces equal 1500 N, so the forces are balanced and acceleration is zero

options.label: B
options.text: Friction is always equal to the driving force, no matter what speed the car travels at

options.label: C
options.text: The car's mass is too large, so the driving force cannot overcome the total friction

options.label: D
options.text: Air resistance increases until it equals half the driving force, stopping acceleration

explanation: At constant speed, the driving force and resistance forces (friction + air resistance) must be equal and opposite, creating balanced forces. When forces are balanced, there is no net force, so acceleration is zero and the car maintains constant speed. Option B is wrong because friction depends on speed; Option C misunderstands how mass and force interact; Option D incorrectly suggests air resistance equals half the driving force.
